message: The party is about to crank in.
It's for showing the faces of the cast.
Yeah, I know that. But, what about the lead not showing up?
Oh, Ted Grayson, huh? I wonder why he’s missing the face show party?
I really hate these things. I want to quickly change out of this outfit into sweats.
You've changed. You used to love parties back in school.
Loving parties is your thing, right? I was there for the girls.
What's that? Then it's the same thing!
If I can't talk to Cindy-chan, I just want to go home.
No, their shine is just different. Cindy is one thing, but Meryl and Jennifer have an overwhelming aura.
Yeah, they're the hottest actresses right now.
Just standing there makes a difference, huh?
Don't you think the room got brighter when those two arrived?
Sure, they're a dazzling pair, but saying the lights got brighter is a weird expression.
I'm actually kind of nervous, believe it or not.
A movie? The crank in hasn’t happened yet. My role is a cool rookie part. Don’t you think it’s a perfect fit?
What, you’re playing a major leaguer in a movie too?
I can’t do anything but baseball. They treat me like a superstar, but it’s a bit annoying.
Saying what you don’t really mean.
Ugh, you see right through me.
After all, it’s the work of a Hollywood master,
Right? To be included is honestly what makes me happy.
That’s not it. Isn’t it really that you’re thrilled to be working with Meryl, Jennifer, and Cindy?
I won’t deny it. But isn’t that how any guy would feel?
I’m really looking forward to the shoot.
When I heard about the movie's content, it felt like it was years later for me.
Is it because of the protagonist's setup?
Yeah. I know a guy who was a great player but fell into drugs after poor performance.
a scary overseer watching over me. I really appreciate that.
Did you face any harassment for being in a movie?
Well, yeah. Even without that, I stand out, right? I actually live a pretty stressful life.
I wonder if I should take this movie as a chance to become an actor.
